24:21 â May 13, 2024
EFM â Risk Severity, No-Risk Reason Clarification and a New No-Risk Reason Added
The Enterprise Fraud Management Part was updated to provide clarification to Risk Severity and No-Risk reasons. Additionally, a new no-risk reason was created to assist with requested cancellations of legitimate
Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
transactions.

Enterprise Fraud Management Part
Section 7.4, Using the Filters Sidebar in the Open Incident List

Section 7.6, Using Incident Filters

We provided minor clarification to various procedures with instructions on how to log in to EFM and access the Incidents page.
Section 8.2, Identifying a Transaction as Fraudulent
We provided instructions on how to log in to EFM and access the Incidents page.
We clarified how to identify a transaction as Risk.
We clarified when a risk severity option is selected.
Section 8.3, Identifying a Transaction as Legitimate
We provided instructions on how to log in to EFM and access the Incidents page.
We clarified how to identify a transaction as No-Risk.
We clarified when a no-risk reason should be selected.
A new no-risk reason was made available to EFM users on May 13, 2024. EFM users can now directly cancel legitimate
Interac
e-Transfer transactions without using the previously provided workaround method.
